research.property_manager: Likely Crossbridge or Del (CentreCourt's usual rotation); confirm at viewing
research.pm_rep_note: CentreCourt is a polarizing dev — known for tight units, premium amenities, on-time delivery. Build quality reviews trending positive in early occupancy.
amenities: 3,500 sq ft Scandinavian spa (hot + cold plunge pools)
200m INDOOR running track (rare, big deal for a 100km/week runner)

review_quotes.quote: Living at Zen has been a really smooth experience ... The gym is one of the better ones seen in any condo and the rooftop is a great bonus in the summer.

Sleek finishes, functional layouts and decent soundproofing ... well-maintained, quiet environment with responsive management, great location, amazing views and top-notch amenities like the gym and pool.
The unit felt a bit tight, with pricing reflecting location and lifestyle rather than square footage.

red_flags: Small floorplate per unit — at $2,700 budget you may end up in 450-500 sqft
Newest building = early-occupancy mgmt teething likely over next 12mo
research.honest_call: STRONGEST PICK on this list for Fadi specifically. The 200m indoor running track + Scandinavian cold plunge + 5,000 sqft gym is unicorn-tier for a 100km/week runner who's also dating-focused. Indoor track means he doesn't lose mileage to Toronto winters. CentreCourt brand = expect tight square footage but premium fit/finish. Walk Score 91, Transit 96. If a unit is in budget, view it first.

research.building_name: Local at Fort York (parcel: 50 Bruyeres + 69 Grand Magazine + 36 Bruyeres)
research.pm_rep_note: Crossbridge here, not FirstService - downgrade vs Yards on PM. Building is mid-rise (less amenity scale).

reddit_quotes.quote: Great location with Loblaws, Shoppers Drug Mart and LCBO nearby.

Past guest reported finding a live bed bug in the guest suite, and after their stay contracted bed bugs which inspection confirmed - 'several bed bug larvae and eggs'. Management reimbursed rental but not personal damage.

google_complaints: one-bedroom units sold without parking included
documented bed bug incident in guest suite (2016-2017)
red_flags: bed bug incident in guest suite is a yellow flag - confirm if remediation was building-wide
1BR units without parking - Fadi has a car, verify parking deeded with any unit
research.honest_call: Solid mid-rise Onni build, but the bed bug history and lack of pool drop it below Yards (20 Bruyeres). If you can get a parking-included unit and verify post-2017 pest history, it is a reasonable pick at $2,700.

amenities.best_run_loop: Step onto the Bentway in 2 min (1.75km traffic-free under Gardiner). Drop south to Martin Goodman at Bathurst Quay (6 min) for 10-15km lake runs. Fort York Historic Site green space immediately adjacent.
amenities.running_insight: Fort York sits between Liberty Village and CityPlace — you can flex either way for run clubs. Bentway directly out the door is unique: traffic-free, lit, all-weather.

research.building_name: Liberty Central by the Lake (Phase I)
research.property_manager: Crossbridge / FirstService Residential (per Liberty Village condo manager directory)
research.pm_rep_note: FirstService acquired Crossbridge April 2023; among the more competent Liberty PMs but high volume across LV portfolio means inconsistent unit-level response.
research.units_floors: approx 385-395 units / 25 floors (twin curved glass towers)

review_quotes.quote: Those with south facing units have views of Lake Ontario, and everyone else gets to enjoy the city skyline.
Aqua massage rooms and a party room with a full spacious kitchen ... pilates studio.

google_complaints: loading dock / move-in bottlenecks common in CanAlfa twin-tower layouts
elevator wait at peak (typical of 385-unit / 25-floor mass)
red_flags: No major systemic red flags surfaced. Generic large-tower friction (elevator peaks, move-in chaos) noted.
research.honest_call: Solid mid-tier Liberty pick. Newer build (2015), south-facing lake views, full amenity stack incl. pool/steam — strong dating optics. 8-10 min walk to King W bars at Strachan, but EAST end of Liberty so the actual Ossington / Trinity Bellwoods scene is a 20-25 min walk or 8 min Uber. Fine for a 27yo attending; not the best-of-class but reliable.

run_clubs.name: Parkdale Roadrunners
run_clubs.meets: Tue 7pm Stay Gold, Sat 9am Bather (450 Dufferin)

amenities.best_run_loop: South to Coronation Park → Martin Goodman Trail (6 min). Humber Bay loop = 12km; High Park via Queen W = ~10km loop. Liberty Village → Parkdale → Roncesvalles is a classic 8km social run.
amenities.running_insight: Same as the other Liberty Village picks — bullseye on East Liberty puts you on top of F45, Metro, and the LV Striders meetup. 100km/week is structurally easy.

research.pm_rep_note: Crossbridge; Montage is one of the more 'fun young' CityPlace addresses.

reddit_quotes.quote: It's still a young crowd in the building, so it's noisy on weekends, though some floors are pretty quiet. Elevators now have key-fob access after 10pm so no one can head up without a fob.

google_complaints: previous elevator issues now reportedly resolved
tiktok_mentions: Montage rooftop pool / BBQ shows up in CityPlace lifestyle TikToks - 'aspirational young condo' branding
red_flags: explicitly called out as a 'noisy on weekends, young crowd' building by UT forum residents
research.honest_call: If Fadi WANTS the social weekend vibe and dates a lot, Montage is actually well-suited - it's a young-professional party-adjacent building. Downside is post-night-shift sleep. Borderline pick - depends on his shift schedule.

amenities.best_run_loop: Martin Goodman Trail access via Bathurst Quay in 4-5 min. Sugar Beach east = 4km; Humber Bay Arch west = 11km. Toronto Music Garden + Music Garden Promenade are gems on the route.
amenities.running_insight: CityPlace canonical address — lake-close, Tribe-close, Sobeys-close. Slightly further from Liberty Village run clubs but BlackToe King West fills that gap.

research.pm_rep_note: FirstService is widely considered the best of the big-three Toronto condo PMs; Onni QA is significantly higher than Concord Adex.

reddit_quotes.quote: Condo walls are very soundproof. Can barely hear cars on the highway or neighbours. Lived here 7 months, condo management responds quickly.
Lived at The Yards for 5 years and thoroughly enjoy it. Gym has full weights and a squat rack.

google_complaints: outdoor patio looks at the highway
smaller amenity set than CityPlace mega-buildings
red_flags: right next to Gardiner Expressway - soundproofing reportedly handles it, but ask to visit during rush hour
smaller amenity set means no pool for off-day swimming
research.honest_call: Strong pick. Onni quality, FirstService management, real squat rack (matters for a 100km/week runner who likely lifts), good soundproofing, mature building. Best 'quiet professional' Fort York option. Highly recommend - get a unit on a high floor NOT facing the Gardiner.

research.pm_rep_note: Smaller building = generally better unit-level service.

review_quotes.quote: Walk Score 94. Located right beside Liberty Village Park ... simpler and smaller than most Liberty Village condos, offering a more intimate feel.

research.google_rating: limited reviews due to small footprint
google_complaints: thin amenity package (no pool, no concierge listed in most summaries)
older 9ft ceilings now standard but stainless appliances aging
red_flags: Amenity-light vs others on the list — no pool, no sauna, no big lounge
research.honest_call: If you DON'T care about pool/spa/big-amenity-stack and want a quieter 201-unit building with a park literally next door for those 100km/week runs, this is interesting. But for a 27yo who dates a lot, the amenity story is too thin vs King West Condos / Liberty Central. The park-adjacency is the unique selling point. Mid-tier pick.

amenities.best_run_loop: South under rail corridor to Coronation Park (5 min) onto Martin Goodman Trail. West to Humber Bay Arch = 10-12km round trip; east to CityPlace = 6km. Liberty Village → Parkdale → High Park loop = 8km classic.
amenities.running_insight: Best of both worlds: in the Liberty Village run-club epicenter (Striders + Parkdale RR + F45 all <20 min) AND 5 min to the lake. King & Bathurst nightlife is a hike (20 min) — Uber it.

research.building_name: King West Condominiums (Phase I of the tri-tower; sometimes labeled as the main King West Condos building)

review_quotes.quote: The Liberty Village area is great with grocery stores, beer store and LCBO all within 1-2 minutes walking distance.

research.honest_call: Closest of the tri-tower to the LCBO / Metro / King St strip — slightly tighter walk to King W bars than 65 or 75. Same amenity package. Fine pick.

research.building_name: King West Condominiums Phase III (Liberty Lakeview Towers complex w/ 75 & 85)
research.property_manager: Crossbridge / FirstService Residential (shared mgmt across the King West tri-tower per LV condo directory)
research.pm_rep_note: Plaza buildings generally well-regarded for construction quality; mgmt criticism is that ONE property manager covers nearly 1,200 suites across three towers — response time can lag.
amenities: billiards / games room (ping pong, foosball, air hockey)

review_quotes.quote: Buyers love the variety of suite sizes, exceptional management, and amenity range that suits every lifestyle. ... The Ossington bus stops right in front of the building for easy TTC access.
Property management could be an issue, with only one main property manager for the entire building.

research.google_rating: not directly surfaced; review aggregators positive-leaning
google_complaints: amenity overcrowding at peak (large tenant pool)
research.honest_call: Best-amenity-per-dollar in Liberty Village for a single 27yo. Bowling alley + virtual golf + sky lounge + pool is genuinely great for dating optics ('come over, we have a bowling alley'). Plaza build = solid. Ossington bus runs from front door = no streetcar dependency. Strong pick.

research.pm_rep_note: Same shared mgmt as Phase III; same complaint about PM thinness.

review_quotes.quote: Good quality construction, and the builder is very quick to address deficiencies, taking pride in their workmanship with issues addressed immediately.

research.honest_call: Functionally identical to 65 East Liberty (same complex, shared amenities). Slightly smaller floorplate (300 vs 425 units) so marginally less crowded elevators. Pick this over 65 if you find a better-priced unit; otherwise they're the same building.

research.pm_rep_note: Liberty Place has surfaced specific 1-star complaints about management responsiveness (see review quotes). FirstService corporate is generally OK but this building's on-site mgmt history is mixed.
amenities: party room w/ landscaped terrace + BBQ

review_quotes.quote: Plumbing issues weekly with massive water damage that remains unrepaired, regular elevator breakdowns ... management couldn't care about the owners and residents less if they tried. 1.0/5.
Residents love the tower's clean design, strong management and unbeatable walkability.

research.google_rating: mixed; 1-star and 5-star both surface, suggesting unit-by-unit experience
google_complaints: security desk located at unused entrance leaving main door unsafe
red_flags: NO swimming pool (despite the size) — surprising omission for amenity-shopping
research.honest_call: Tallest tower in the cluster (32 floors, Flatiron design — high views are stunning), but reputation is the most damaged of the bunch. Weekly plumbing leak reports + no pool + accessibility lawsuit allegations = avoid unless you find a deeply-discounted high-floor unit and you're OK gambling on which side of management you get. Not the first choice for a $2,700/mo attending.

run_clubs.name: Parkdale Roadrunners
run_clubs.meets: Tue 7pm Stay Gold, Sat 9am Bather (450 Dufferin)

amenities.best_run_loop: Coronation Park → Martin Goodman in ~6 min. Humber Bay out-and-back = 12km. Easy access to Parkdale loops + High Park (3.5km away, then 4km perimeter = 11km loop total).
amenities.running_insight: Western-most Liberty Village = closest to Parkdale Roadrunners' Saturday meetup and High Park, but furthest from King W nightlife. For a 100km/week runner this trade is worth it; for socializing it isn't.
